2604.04878.title: Learning, Potential, and Retention: An Approach for Evaluating Adaptive AI-Enabled Medical Devices
2604.04878.paper_summary: This work addresses challenges in evaluating adaptive artificial intelligence (AI) models for medical devices, where iterative updates to both models and evaluation datasets complicate performance assessment. We introduce a novel approach with three complementary measurements: learning (model improvement on current data), potential (dataset-driven performance shifts), and retention (knowledge preservation across modification steps), to disentangle performance changes caused by model adaptations versus dynamic environments. Case studies using simulated population shifts demonstrate the approach's utility: gradual transitions enable stable learning and retention, while rapid shifts reveal trade-offs between plasticity and stability. These measurements provide practical insights for regulatory science, enabling rigorous assessment of the safety and effectiveness of adaptive AI systems over sequential modifications.
2604.04878.paper_summary_zh: 這項工作針對評估醫療設備的自適應人工智慧（AI）模型所面臨的挑戰，因為模型和評估數據集的迭代更新使性能評估變得複雜。我們提出了一種新穎的方法，包含三個互補的測量指標：學習（模型在當前數據上的改進）、潛力（基於數據集的性能變化）和保留（在修改步驟中知識的保存），以區分由模型適應與動態環境引起的性能變化。使用模擬人口變化的案例研究展示了該方法的實用性：逐漸的過渡促進了穩定的學習和保留，而快速的變化則揭示了可塑性與穩定性之間的權衡。這些測量指標為監管科學提供了實用的見解，使得能夠對自適應AI系統在連續修改過程中的安全性和有效性進行嚴格評估。
2604.04878.author: Alexis Burgon et.al.
2604.04878.authors: Alexis Burgon, Berkman Sahiner, Nicholas A Petrick, Gene Pennello, Ravi K Samala
